channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: NextDecade Seeks to Raise $3.5B in Debt Instrument Sale for Rio Grande LNG
item.description: NextDecade is offering a series of senior secured notes with a total principal amount of $3.5 billion to fund the under-construction Rio Grande LNG project in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: Equinor to transfer Peon interest to Vår Energi in NCS deal
item.description: Equinor and Vår Energi have agreed to exchange assets on the NCS in a deal intended to advance the Peon gas discovery toward development and strengthen Equinor’s position in the Troll–Fram area.

item.title: India Plans to Cut Reliance on Middle East Oil
item.description: India's state oil refiners are planning to reduce their reliance on the Middle East following the supply shock of the Iran war, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Who Is the World's Biggest Oil Producer?
item.description: Rigzone examines the Energy Institute's latest statistical review of world energy report, which was released recently.

item.title: NLNG awards Baker Hughes 13-year gas turbine services contract
item.description: Baker Hughes has been awarded a 13-year contract by Nigeria LNG (NLNG) to deliver comprehensive lifecycle services for turbomachinery at the latter’s liquefaction facility in Bonny Island, Nigeria.

item.title: Eni, NOC start production from Libya’s Sabratha Compression Project
item.description: Eni and the Libyan National Oil Corporation (NOC), operating together via the Mellitah Oil & Gas joint venture, have begun hydrocarbon production from the Sabratha Compression Project.

item.title: Low $70s Not a Fair Price for Oil, Analyst Outlines
item.description: 'One would assume that a fair price today would be more like $80-90 per barrel than low $70s', SEB Chief Commodities Analyst Bjarne Schieldrop said.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Cuts Brent Forecast to $75 a Barrel
item.description: Morgan Stanley has slashed its oil price forecasts for the next 18 month as it expects the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to accelerate a new supply glut. The return of oil supply from the Middle East, combined with…

item.title: San Mateo Midstream to acquire Cardinal Midstream for $752m
item.description: San Mateo Midstream has agreed to acquire the operating subsidiaries of rival US-based midstream energy company Cardinal Midstream Partners for $752m in cash.

item.title: Asian Refiners Redirect Middle East Crude to the U.S. as Hormuz Flows Recover
item.description: Some Asian refiners have recently offered Middle Eastern cargoes to the U.S. West Coast as supply from the Persian Gulf rises with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, while Asian buyers are well-supplied for the next…

item.title: TechnipFMC Bags Large Contract to Deliver New Var Energi Projects
item.description: 'This direct award follows a five-year collaboration agreement signed in 2025 to deliver subsea projects utilizing TechnipFMC's integrated model and accelerate development through coordinated portfolio execution'.

item.title: Uniper Signs Its First Offshore Wind Power Purchase Agreement
item.description: Uniper agreed to buy 100 megawatts from the future Gennaker offshore wind farm on Germany's side of the Baltic Sea for an extendable 10 years.

item.title: Eni Adds Gas Production Capacity in Libya
item.description: Italy's Eni and Libya's NOC started up a compression project that improves natural gas recovery in the Bahr Essalam field off the Mediterranean coast of the North African country.

item.title: Iran Steps Up Talk of Keeping Control of Hormuz
item.description: A top Iranian official reiterated the country's determination to maintain control over maritime traffic moving through the Strait of Hormuz, raising the stakes ahead of fresh negotiations with the U.S. on ending their…

item.title: Hormuz Tanker Traffic Recovers as Tensions Ease
item.description: Tanker traffic at the Strait of Hormuz rose over the past 24 hours for the first time since late last week, when two attacks on commercial vessels spooked many operators and forced others to wait for the easing of the…

item.title: Pakistan Pays Premium for Urgent LNG Cargo
item.description: Pakistan has bought a cargo of liquefied natural gas, paying a premium of around $1 per million British thermal units to regional spot market prices in a move highlighting the continued disruption in LNG flows out of…

item.title: New Report Says U.S. Led Global CO2 Emissions Growth in 2025
item.description: A report has criticized the United States for leading global carbon dioxide emissions growth in 2025, saying the country accounted for around 30% of the global increase in emissions. The rise in emissions came as a…

item.title: Trump Pressures Gas Stations to Slash Prices "Immediately"
item.description: President Donald Trump has scolded fuel retailers for failing to cut prices at the pump, saying in a social media post “Gasoline Retailers must get their Prices down, IMMEDIATELY! They’re too high considering that Oil…

item.title: Putin Acknowledges Russia Faces Fuel Supply Issues
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said fuel supply problems persist for motorists and businesses.

item.title: Oil Rises as Peace Talks Resume
item.description: Crude climbed after the US and Iran signaled renewed diplomacy while investors monitored shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.
